expectations.text: Output includes a coverage matrix mapping design elements to test cases
expectations.evidence: coverage_matrix.md is present with detailed tables mapping design elements to test cases across 4 strategies (Requirement-Based, Equivalence Class, Boundary Value, Error Handling). Total 63 test cases, 16 design elements covered.

expectations.text: Every behavior rule (startup clamping, cruise rate-of-change, emergency shutdown, was_clamped flag) has at least one test
expectations.evidence: Startup clamping: test_startup_mode_clamps_rate_to_min_when_below, test_startup_mode_clamps_rate_to_max_when_above, test_startup_mode_allows_rate_within_bounds. Cruise rate-of-change: test_cruise_mode_enforces_rate_of_change_limit_on_increase, test_cruise_mode_enforces_rate_of_change_limit_on_decrease, test_cruise_mode_allows_rate_change_within_limit. Emergency shutdown: test_emergency_shutdown_sets_rate_to_zero_regardless_of_requested. was_clamped: test_was_clamped_true_when_actual_differs_from_requested, test_was_clamped_false_when_actual_equals_requested.

expectations.text: Every error_handling entry (negative rate, negative elapsed_time, unrecognized mode) has a dedicated test
expectations.evidence: Negative rate: test_error_negative_requested_rate_treated_as_zero (dedicated test, passes). Negative elapsed_time: test_error_negative_elapsed_time_treated_as_zero (dedicated test, passes). Unrecognized mode: test_error_unrecognized_mode_fails_safe_to_emergency exists but does NOT actually test with an unrecognized mode value -- it passes OperationalMode.EMERGENCY_SHUTDOWN (a valid mode) instead of an invalid/unrecognized value (line 729). This test does not verify the fail-safe behavior for unrecognized modes.

expectations.text: Boundary values tested for requested_rate (0.0, negative, at STARTUP_MIN, at STARTUP_MAX, at CRUISE_MAX)
expectations.evidence: test_requested_rate_at_zero_boundary (0.0), test_requested_rate_just_below_zero_invalid (-0.1, negative), test_requested_rate_at_startup_min_boundary (10.0), test_requested_rate_at_startup_max_boundary (50.0), test_requested_rate_at_cruise_max_boundary (200.0). All boundary values are present.

expectations.text: No test uses 'assert does not raise' or 'assert no exception' as its only assertion
expectations.evidence: Every test includes concrete assertions on result.actual_rate, result.was_clamped, and/or result.clamping_reason. No test relies solely on absence-of-exception assertions.

expectations.text: Test names describe the scenario being tested, not just the method name
expectations.evidence: Test names follow descriptive scenario patterns, e.g. test_startup_mode_clamps_rate_to_min_when_below, test_cruise_mode_enforces_rate_of_change_limit_on_increase, test_emergency_shutdown_sets_rate_to_zero_regardless_of_requested, test_was_clamped_true_when_actual_differs_from_requested. None are named like test_limit or test_clamp.

expectations.text: Stateful behavior tested: rate-of-change limiting requires multiple sequential calls
expectations.evidence: Multiple tests use sequential calls: test_previous_rate_tracks_across_calls (2 calls verifying rate-of-change), test_multiple_calls_in_sequence_startup_to_cruise (3 sequential calls), test_cruise_mode_enforces_rate_of_change_limit_on_increase (setup call + test call). TestStatefulBehavior class is dedicated to this.

expectations.text: Configuration variants tested: at least one test with non-default config values
expectations.evidence: The FuelRateLimiter class uses class-level constants (STARTUP_MIN_RATE=10.0, STARTUP_MAX_RATE=50.0, etc.) with no constructor parameter for customization. All tests use default config values. The coverage matrix explicitly states 'Configuration customization tests are out of scope for this test suite.' No test uses non-default config values.
